breakfast.name: Masaledar Anda Bhurji with Toasted Bread
breakfast.description: A quick and spicy Indian-style scrambled eggs, packed with flavors from onions, green chilies, and basic spices. Served with crisp, lightly toasted whole wheat bread, it's a hearty and low-effort start to your day, perfectly using up those pantry eggs and onions.
instructions: Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add chopped onion and green chili. Sauté until onions turn translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder (if using), and salt. Cook for 30 seconds.
Crack eggs directly into the pan or whisk them lightly in a bowl before adding. Scramble continuously until cooked through but still moist.
Stir in chopped coriander leaves. Remove from heat.
Lightly toast the whole wheat bread. Serve the Anda Bhurji hot with the toasted bread.

lunch.name: Comforting Jeera Rice with Aloo Pyaz ki Sukhi Sabzi
lunch.description: A classic, homely combination featuring fragrant Jeera Rice and a simple, dry stir-fry of potatoes and onions. The sabzi is spiced lightly for comfort, with a hint of tang from amchur. This meal efficiently utilizes your potatoes and onions, offering a satisfying and quick lunch.
instructions: Wash rice thoroughly and soak for 15 minutes while you prep. Drain.

For Jeera Rice: In a small pot, heat 1/2 tsp oil. Add 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, let splutter. Add drained rice and 2 cups water, salt to taste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 12-15 minutes until water is absorbed and rice is tender.
For Aloo Pyaz Sabzi: Heat remaining oil in a pan. Add remaining cumin seeds, let them splutter.
Add chopped onion and sauté until golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add diced potatoes, turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Cover the pan and cook on low heat, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender (about 10-12 minutes). If sticking, add a splash of water.
Once potatoes are cooked, add amchur powder and mix well. Sauté for another 2 minutes without lid until slightly crispy.
Garnish sabzi with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with Jeera Rice.

dinner.name: Quick Home-style Chicken Curry with Steamed Rice
dinner.description: A rustic and flavorful chicken curry cooked in a fragrant onion-tomato gravy, perfect for a cozy dinner. This recipe focuses on fresh spices and quick preparation to fit your time constraints, ensuring a tender and delicious chicken dish that makes the most of your chicken and onions.
instructions: Wash and drain chicken pieces. Season with a pinch of salt and turmeric powder.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and bay leaf; let them splutter.
Add chopped onion and sauté until deep golden brown, about 7-9 minutes. This step is crucial for flavor.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1-2 minutes until the raw smell disappears.
Add chopped tomatoes (or puree) and cook, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes soften and oil starts to separate, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes, adding a splash of water if the masala sticks.
Add the seasoned chicken pieces to the pan. Mix well, ensuring chicken is coated with the masala. Cook for 5-7 minutes on medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until chicken changes color.
Pour in 1 cup of water, bring to a simmer. Cover the pan and cook on low heat for 10-12 minutes, or until chicken is tender and cooked through.

While curry cooks, wash rice thoroughly. In a separate pot, add rice and 2 cups water, a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and cook for 12-15 minutes until water is absorbed and rice is tender.
Once chicken is cooked, stir in garam masala. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
Serve hot with steamed Basmati rice.

cookingTodoList.instruction: Finely chop 1 small onion and 1 green chili for Anda Bhurji. Chop 1 tbsp coriander leaves for garnish.
Start cooking Anda Bhurji in a pan. Simultaneously, toast 2 slices of whole wheat bread.
Serve Masaledar Anda Bhurji with Toasted Bread.
Wash 1 cup Basmati Rice and soak for 15 minutes. In parallel, dice 2 medium potatoes and finely chop 1 large onion for Aloo Pyaz ki Sabzi. Chop 1 tbsp coriander leaves for garnish.
Drain soaked rice. Start cooking Jeera Rice in a small pot. Begin preparing Aloo Pyaz ki Sabzi in a separate pan.
Finish cooking and serve Comforting Jeera Rice with Aloo Pyaz ki Sukhi Sabzi.

Finely chop 1 large onion and 2 medium tomatoes for Chicken Curry. Prepare 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste (or finely mince ginger and garlic). Cut 300g chicken into small pieces. Chop 2 tbsp coriander leaves for garnish. Wash 1 cup Basmati Rice.
Start cooking Home-style Chicken Curry in a heavy-bottomed pan, focusing on browning onions and cooking masala.
While the chicken curry simmers, start cooking Steamed Rice in a separate pot.
Finish cooking and serve Quick Home-style Chicken Curry with Steamed Rice.

items.name: Coriander Leaves
items.substitution: Mint leaves or dried fenugreek leaves (Kasoori Methi)

items.name: Tomatoes
items.substitution: Tomato puree (2-3 tbsp) or canned diced tomatoes

items.name: Coriander Powder
items.substitution: Cumin powder (if no other option, alter flavor)

items.name: Amchur Powder (Dry Mango Powder)
items.substitution: Lemon juice (a dash) or tamarind paste (small amount)

items.name: Bay Leaf
items.substitution: Green Cardamom pod (very small amount, for aroma)

items.name: Basmati Rice
items.substitution: Any long-grain rice like Sona Masuri or Kolam rice

breakfast.name: Zesty Masala Anda Bhurji
breakfast.description: A vibrant and quick Indian-style scrambled eggs, packed with the fresh punch of finely chopped onions and green chilies, seasoned with turmeric and a hint of red chili. Perfect for a speedy, protein-rich start to your day.
instructions: Finely chop the onion and green chili.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add chopped onions and green chilies, sauté until translucent, about 2-3 minutes.
Crack eggs into a bowl, add turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt. Whisk lightly.
Pour the egg mixture into the pan with the sautéed onions.
Scramble the eggs with a spatula, stirring continuously until cooked through but still moist, about 3-4 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve immediately.

lunch.name: Rustic Aloo Pyaz Sabzi with Fluffy Steamed Rice
lunch.description: A simple yet profoundly satisfying dry potato and onion stir-fry, seasoned with everyday Indian spices like turmeric, red chili, and cumin. Paired with fluffy steamed rice, it's the epitome of comforting home-cooked Indian food – easy to make, light on the stomach, and rich in traditional flavors.
instructions: Wash and soak rice for 10 minutes. In parallel, peel and dice potatoes into small cubes. Thinly slice the onion.
For rice: Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a pot. Add soaked and drained rice, a pinch of salt. Cover and simmer on low heat for 15-18 minutes until water is absorbed and rice is cooked.
For sabzi: Heat oil in a separate pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Add diced potatoes, turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well.
Cover the pan and cook on low-medium heat, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender, about 10-12 minutes. Add a splash of water if sticking.
Once potatoes are cooked, increase heat slightly and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly crisp.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

dinner.name: Hearty Aloo Matar Pulao with Cooling Dahi
dinner.description: A fragrant and flavorful one-pot rice dish, featuring tender potatoes and sweet green peas cooked with aromatic whole spices like bay leaf and cinnamon, along with a hint of ginger-garlic. This wholesome pulao is a complete meal in itself, perfectly balanced with a side of cooling fresh curd.
instructions: Wash and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes. Peel and dice potato into medium cubes. Slice onion thinly.
Heat oil or ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot or pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, cloves, and green cardamom. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown, about 4-5 minutes.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for another minute until the raw smell disappears.
Add diced potatoes and green peas. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the pot. Gently mix with the vegetables for 1 minute.

Pour in 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 15-18 minutes until water is absorbed and rice is tender. If using a pressure cooker, cook for 1 whistle on medium heat, then let pressure release naturally.
Fluff the pulao gently with a fork. Serve hot with fresh curd.

cookingTodoList.instruction: Chop 1 small onion and 1 green chili for Anda Bhurji.
Heat oil in a pan, add chopped onions and green chilies, sauté until translucent (2-3 min).
Whisk eggs with turmeric, red chili powder, and salt. Pour into the pan.
Scramble eggs continuously until cooked through (3-4 min).
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve.
Wash and soak 1 cup Basmati rice. Peel and dice 2 medium potatoes into small cubes. Thinly slice 1 medium onion.
Start cooking rice: bring 2 cups water to boil, add drained rice and pinch of salt, cover and simmer (15-18 min).
In a separate pan, heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter (1 min).
Add sliced onions to pan and sauté until golden brown (3-4 min).
Add diced potatoes, turmeric, red chili, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well (2 min).
Cover the pan and cook on low-medium heat, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender (10-12 min).
Increase heat slightly, stir-fry potatoes for 2-3 minutes until slightly crisp. Serve with steamed rice.
Wash and soak 1 cup Basmati rice (for 20 mins). Peel and dice 1 large potato. Slice 1 medium onion. Measure 1/2 cup green peas.
Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, cloves, green cardamom, sauté (30 sec).
Add sliced onions and sauté until golden brown (4-5 min).
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1 minute.
Add diced potatoes and green peas. Cook for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well (1 min).
Drain soaked rice, add to pot, and gently mix with vegetables (1 min).
Pour in 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until water is absorbed and rice is tender (15-18 min).
Fluff the pulao gently with a fork. Serve hot with fresh curd.

items.name: Fresh Curd (Dahi)
items.substitution: Lemon juice for tang (not a direct substitute)
